exemplar.title: How does the distance to a galaxy affect the calculated value of the Hubble constant?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the distance to a galaxy affect the calculated value of the Hubble constant?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The title and research question are clearly stated and effectively set the investigationâs focus.
Background sections on redâshift, Doppler shift, and Hubbleâs law are logically organized and support the studyâs purpose.
Methodology demonstrates informed selection of data sources and relevant calculations (e.g., unit conversion, relativistic Doppler equation).
weaknesses: Minor phrasing errors (e.g., conflating emitted vs. observed wavelength symbols) occasionally reduce precision.
The scope drifts somewhat in later sections (claims about cosmic acceleration beyond the dataâs capacity).
Justification of sampling strategy and choice of bin widths is described but lacks sufficient detail and bias mitigation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates its topic and research question with clarity and purpose and employs an appropriate range of methods, meriting a mark in the top band. However, occasional imprecision in terminology and a drifting focus in analysis prevent a flawless performance at the very top of the band (6). A score of 5 reflects a strong, coherent focus and generally complete methodology, with room for tighter precision and scope control.

strengths: Demonstrates solid understanding of cosmological concepts (relativistic Doppler shift, Hubbleâs law) and correctly applies them in most calculations.
Uses specialist terminology (e.g., ârecessional velocity,â âabsolute uncertaintyâ) appropriately, showing clear subject knowledge.
Integrates relevant sources coherently to support theoretical explanations.
weaknesses: Occasional numerical inconsistencies (typo in lightâyear to parsec conversion) introduce systematic error.
Variable symbols (Î»â vs. Î»_emitted) are sometimes conflated, reducing clarity.
Some axis labels are misâlabelled, indicating lapses in careful application of terminology.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good subject knowledge and generally accurate use of terminology, fitting the upper middle of the 6-mark band. Occasional calculation typos and inconsistent symbol usage prevent a topâband performance. A score of 4 reflects solid understanding with targeted areas for correction.

strengths: Analysis includes multiple data fits and gradient calculations that are clearly connected to the research question.
Argument progression is logicalâfrom theoretical background through data processing to evaluation and conclusion.
Discussion acknowledges non-linearity, outliers, and catalogue inconsistencies, demonstrating critical engagement with the results.
weaknesses: Error propagation is treated heuristically rather than through formal statistical methods.
Conclusions about cosmic acceleration extend beyond what the dataset and methods can rigorously support.
Quantitative evaluation (e.g., RÂ² reporting, treatment of outliers) remains superficial and could be strengthened.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation presents good, clearly relevant analysis and a coherent, wellâdeveloped argument indicative of the low end of the 7â9 band. While not yet exhibiting the sophistication or rigor of the top band (10â12), it surpasses the 4â6 band thresholds through its structured critical engagement and multiple analytic approaches. A mark of 7 appropriately rewards this level of critical thinking.

strengths: The report follows EE structural conventions (title page, table of contents, numbered sections) comprehensively.
Visual elements (graphs, tables, images) are integrated to support the text and aid comprehension.
Formatting (section headings, citation list) is consistent and professional throughout.
weaknesses: Figure numbering is occasionally inconsistent and some captions are truncated.
A few images are low resolution and could be clearer.
Appendix figures lack context and would benefit from integration into the main discussion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the presentation is clear, wellâstructured, and adheres to all standard EE conventions, supporting effective communication. Minor formatting and caption issues prevent a flawless score but do not detract significantly from readability. A full mark of 4 reflects strong performance in structure and layout.

strandSelections.reasoning: Specialist terms such as ârecessional velocityâ, âabsolute uncertaintyâ, âinverse exponential fitâ are used largely correctly, but the essay occasionally conflates symbols (Î»â vs Î»_emitted) and mis-labels axes.  Terminology is therefore mostly, but not flawlessly, accurateâconsistent with 3 marks in the 3-4 band.

The argument moves logically from background theory to data processing, then to evaluation and conclusion.  Reasoning is coherent, but claims about dark-energy acceleration stretch beyond evidenced data, and error discussion is qualitative.  Thus the argument is well-developed but not sophisticatedâmid-band 4-6 with 5 marks.

The student selects a publicly available catalogue, converts units, calculates velocities with a relativistic Doppler equation and segments data for gradient determination.  However, justification of sampling strategy, error propagation and choice of bin widths is superficial, and the data setâs heterogeneous provenance is acknowledged but not mitigated.  Evidence of informed methodological choice is present but incomplete, fitting the middle of band 3-4.

The essay demonstrates solid grasp of cosmological conceptsârelativistic Doppler shift, red-shift, Hubbleâs lawâand applies them correctly in most calculations.  Occasional numerical inconsistencies (e.g. light-year to Mpc conversion typo) and an over-interpretation of results prevent a âconsistently excellentâ judgement, so upper-middle band is appropriate.

The evaluation section notes catalogue inconsistencies and increasing error bars, representing some critical perspective, yet offers little quantitative assessment or alternative approaches.  Reflection on model limitations (e.g. cosmological parameters) is brief.  This level of critique is âgoodâ but not thorough, meriting the lower end of band 4-6.

The title, research question, and introductory section clearly establish that the study investigates how calculated Hubble-constant values vary with galactic distance.  Background subsections outline red-shift, Doppler shift and Hubbleâs law, showing a logical focus.  Minor lapses in phrasing (e.g. conflating emitted and observed wavelength symbols) occasionally cloud precision, so the communication, while strong, is not flawless; hence the lower end of the top band (5).

Graphs, tables and images are integrated and generally labelled; error bars are visible.  Visual clarity is adequate though several images are low-resolution and some captions are truncated.  Layout aids comprehension despite imperfections, warranting 3 marks in the top presentation band.

The research question is explicitly stated and generally narrow, yet later analysis strays into claims about cosmic acceleration that the chosen data set and method cannot verify, indicating only partial focus.  Connection to the body of the essay remains adequate, but the drifting scope prevents the very high focus required for band 5-6.

Data are graphed and gradients extracted; the student recognises non-linearity and attempts several fits, showing relevant analysis.  Yet statistical treatment is limited to simple averages and maxima/minima; propagation of red-shift uncertainties into velocity is crude.  Overall analysis is clear and relevant but not rigorous enough for bands 7-9, placing it mid-band 4-6.

The essay follows EE structural conventions with title page, table of contents, numbered sections, headings and citation list.  Minor omissions (inconsistent figure numbering, some duplicated table rows) reduce polish but overall structure supports communicationâupper band 3-4, assigning 3 marks.

annotations.comment: The title and stated research question demonstrate clear focus and relevance to the investigationâs purpose, effectively setting the context for the reader.
The phrasing conflates ârestâ and âemittedâ wavelength symbols (<math>\lambda_0</math> vs. <math>\lambda_{emitted}</math>), reducing precision. Clarify definitions and usage.
Table of contents is comprehensive, but some subâsections lack corresponding page numbers. Ensure every entry maps clearly to its page.
The introduction provides historical context but lacks citation for Big Bang theory formulation. Include a primary source reference to strengthen knowledge demonstration.
Equation 1 correctly shows the relativistic Doppler formula, but the item list mislabels âemitted frequencyâ without prime notation. Align variable symbols for consistency.
The explanation of redshift introduces qualitative factors (dark energy, light travel time) without scope alignment. Restrict discussion to methodology or move advanced concepts to evaluation.
The conversion factor uses â3.36156Ã10^6â instead of â3.26156Ã10^6â for lightâyear to parsec. Correct this to avoid systematic errors in all subsequent distance values.
The sampling strategy is described as systematic random but lacks detail on interval selection and handling of duplicates. Expand on how bias was minimized in Data Collection.
The raw data table shows duplicated entries for UDFj-39546284. Verify and clean the dataset to avoid skewing analysis.
The uncertainty propagation into velocity is treated heuristically. A formal errorâpropagation formula should be applied to combine redshift and distance uncertainties.
Some figure captions (e.g., Fig. 3) are truncated and lack source details. Ensure all captions follow a consistent format and include source/reference.
Graph 1 analysis omits reporting of fit quality (RÂ²). Include statistical metrics for each regression to support claims of relationship form.
Figure 7âs integration of parallax discussion interrupts data processing. Move this to background or appendix to maintain methodological focus.
The segmentation of distance ranges for gradient calculation is justified, yet criteria for section boundaries are arbitrary. Provide rationale or test alternative binning.
Averaging maximum and minimum gradients yields midrange rather than central tendency. Discuss merits of this choice versus least-squares uncertainty.
The summary table in Figure 10 presents outlier gradients (e.g., 138.2, 171.5) without comment. Evaluate these in the discussion and consider their impact on overall trend.
The conclusion overinterprets observational Hâ variation as cosmic acceleration. Data and method cannot isolate dark energy effects; temper conclusions accordingly.
The evaluation acknowledges heterogeneity of data sources but does not propose mitigation. Suggest using homogeneous subsamples or weighting by instrument uncertainties.
Citation styles in the Works Cited are inconsistent (missing authorsâ names, italics, retrieval dates). Adopt a uniform style guide (e.g., MLA, APA).
Figures in the appendix occupy entire pages without captions or context. Remove redundant blank tables or integrate key plots into main text with commentary.
